name: Admission & Discharge Extractor
description: Extract structured patient and clinical metadata from admission/discharge documents and medical records

prompt.role: You are a medical records specialist skilled in extracting and structuring administrative and clinical data from medical documents.
prompt.task: Extract key patient, provider, and clinical information from medical documents and discharge summaries.
rules: Extract ONLY information explicitly stated in the document
Do NOT invent or assume missing data
If a value is not found in the document, use the default value provided.
For dates, use ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) or the exact format found in document
extraction_keys: doctor_name: Full name of the attending physician or primary provider (if multiple providers listed, use the primary or attending)
provider_id: The provider's identifier, credentials ID, or license number (use format as found in document, or omit if truly absent)
patient_name: Patient's full name as documented
patient_id: Patient's medical record number (MRN), social security number, or hospital ID (use masked format if needed)
patient_gender: Male / Female / Other / Not specified
patient_age: Age in years at time of admission; if not provided, calculate from DOB if available
admitted_date: Date of hospital/facility admission (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD preferred)
discharge_date: Date of discharge or release from facility
discharged_to: Discharge destination (e.g., 'Home', 'Rehabilitation Facility', 'Skilled Nursing Facility', 'Home with home health services', 'Deceased')
drug_allergies: List of known drug allergies. Format as comma-separated string or array. Use 'NKDA' (No Known Drug Allergies) if explicitly stated or if no allergies are documented
patient_medical_history: Patient's past medical history (PMHx) - chronic conditions, previous illnesses, and significant past medical events. Format as comma-separated list or brief narrative

family_medical_history: Family's medical history (FHx) - significant heritable or familial conditions mentioned. Use 'None' or 'Not documented' if not mentioned in document (do NOT assume or invent family history)
social_habits: Patient's social history (SHx) - smoking status, alcohol use, substance use, living situation. Use 'None' or 'Not documented' if not mentioned (do NOT assume)
output_requirements: Return valid {output_format} with all keys present
String values for all fields (no nested objects except where array is appropriate for allergies/history)
Empty or default values must still include the key
For lists (allergies, medical history), use semicolon-separated strings or {output_format} arrays or lists
Dates: ISO format YYYY-MM-DD, or exact format from document if different
Return ONLY valid {output_format} with the specified keys and code fences

form.title: Admission & Discharge Data Extraction
form.description: Review the extracted structured metadata from your medical document. The AI has parsed patient demographics, provider information, admission/discharge details, and clinical history into a standardized {output_format} format.

fields.name: text
fields.label: Medical Document Text
fields.help: Paste the full text of a medical document, discharge summary, admission record, or clinical note. The tool will extract patient demographics, provider info, dates, allergies, and medical history into structured {output_format} format. Maximum 1,000,000 characters.
fields.placeholder:
Example:
ADMISSION RECORD
Doctor: Dr. James Smith, MD (Provider ID: PS-12345)
Patient: John Doe
MRN: 123-45-6789
Age: 65 years old, Male
Admitted: 2024-03-15
Discharge Date: 2024-03-22
Discharge Destination: Home with home health services

Drug Allergies: Penicillin (rash), Sulfonamides
PMH: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, CAD status post stent 2020
FHx: Father with MI at age 60, Mother with diabetes
SHx: Former smoker (quit 2010), occasional alcohol, retired engineer
